name: Porta Cabin Shop
description:
Porta Cabin Shop — Factory-Built Retail Outlet, Ready in Days

A porta cabin shop from SAMAN is a factory-configured retail outlet — not a plain site cabin with a door added. Before your customer sets foot inside, they see your frontage. That is where a shop cabin succeeds or fails as a commercial outlet, and it is the first decision to make.

What Your Customer Sees — Frontage Options for a Porta Cabin Shop

The front face of your cabin is your shop front. SAMAN factory-fits one of four frontage configurations before dispatch — chosen at the order stage, not retrofitted on site.

Full-width fixed glass panel: A 10mm toughened glass panel spanning the full front face width — typically 8 to 10 feet — with an aluminium frame and a hinged glass entry door at one end. Maximum product display visibility. Used for retail outlets, pharmacies, and small supermarkets where the customer needs to see inside before entering.

Sliding aluminium service counter window: A counter-height sliding aluminium window with a transaction shelf and a security grille option. No customer entry required. Used for kiosks, ticketing counters, petrol station ancillary shops, and any service format where the operator works from inside and the customer transacts from outside.

Hinged glass entry door with fixed side panels: A central hinged glass door flanked by fixed glass panels either side — total glazed area approximately 50% of the front face. Customer entry through the centre. Used for franchise counters, food outlets, and walk-in service shops where browsing and customer movement inside are part of the format.

Signage panel and roller shutter: A flat composite signage panel mounted above a full-width roller shutter. Cabin is fully closed and secured when the roller shutter is down. Used for highway roadside shops, market stalls, and any retail format that operates specific hours and needs physical security when closed.

Full-width toughened glass frontage on a SAMAN porta cabin shop — pharmacy configuration with product display shelving visible inside. Factory-fitted glass panel and aluminium frame before dispatch. Four frontage configurations available: fixed glass, counter window, entry door with side panels, or roller shutter with signage panel.

Frontage type is confirmed at the order stage. Changing the frontage on site after delivery costs significantly more than factory configuration — confirm before we fabricate.

Branding Your Shop Cabin — Signage, Colour, and Wrap Options

Every SAMAN porta cabin shop leaves the factory prepared for branding. Three elements are confirmed at the order stage.

Signage panel above the entry: A flat mild steel or composite panel — typically 2 to 3 feet tall running the full cabin width — is fabricated above the frontage as a standard provision on every shop configuration. The panel is primed and smooth-finished, ready for vinyl-printed or backlit brand signage installed by your sign contractor after delivery.

Custom RAL colour cladding: The exterior corrugated steel wall panels are colour-coated to any standard RAL chart colour at the factory. If your franchise or brand has a corporate colour — RAL 1021 Traffic Yellow, RAL 5010 Gentian Blue, RAL 3020 Traffic Red — we match it at the powder coat stage. No field painting required.

Vinyl wrap provision: If your brand identity requires a large-format graphic wrap rather than a solid colour — a franchise livery, a product image, or a branded pattern — the exterior panels are prepared with a smooth primer coat suitable for direct vinyl application. Vinyl is supplied and applied by your branding contractor on site.

A SAMAN porta cabin shop with custom RAL colour-coated cladding matching a franchise brand identity — saffron orange corrugated steel panels, white backlit signage fascia, counter service window. Custom RAL colour applied at the factory powder coat stage before dispatch.

For buyers who want to know what material sits under the branded cladding, our ms porta cabin page covers the IS 2062 Grade A frame specification in detail.

Which Size Fits Your Retail Format

Retail Format Recommended Size Configuration
Single-staff kiosk, counter service 6×8 ft Sliding counter window, no customer entry
Small retail outlet, 2–3 customers inside 10×10 ft Glass entry door + side panels
Food outlet or pharmacy with shelving 10×20 ft Full-width glass + entry door
Franchise service counter with back office 12×20 ft Glass front + internal partition
Multi-section convenience store 20×20 ft Full glass front + roller shutter
Petrol station ancillary shop 10×15 ft Sliding counter or glass entry

SAMAN porta cabin shop sizes side by side — a 6×8 ft counter-service kiosk (left) and a 10×20 ft glass-entry retail outlet (right). The size difference is immediate. Recommended size depends on format: counter service, walk-in retail, or multi-staff pharmacy. Six formats mapped to sizes in the guide above.

These are the most commonly ordered configurations. Custom dimensions and layouts are available — confirm your format and approximate customer count at the quote stage and we will recommend the right size before fabricating.

Porta Cabin Shop vs Permanent Brick Outlet — Cost and Time

A 10×10 ft SAMAN porta cabin shop with glass frontage, branded colour cladding, and signage panel ready — costs Rs 1,80,000 to Rs 2,50,000 and is operational within 7 to 21 working days of order confirmation.

An equivalent permanent brick-and-mortar shop of the same floor area — including construction, plastering, tiling, electrical, and basic shopfitting — costs Rs 4,00,000 to Rs 6,00,000 and takes 3 to 4 months from foundation to opening.

The porta cabin shop generates revenue 2.5 to 3 months earlier than an equivalent permanent outlet. For a franchise operator opening multiple locations, or a business testing a new location before committing to a permanent lease improvement, the time saving is the primary commercial argument — not just the cost saving.

The porta cabin is also a movable asset. If the location underperforms, the cabin moves. A permanent brick shop does not.

The same commercial footprint — the SAMAN porta cabin shop is open and trading (left) while the equivalent brick shop next door is still under construction (right). A 10×10 ft porta cabin shop is operational in 7 to 21 working days. An equivalent brick structure takes 3 to 4 months.

Shop and Establishment Compliance — What You Need to Know

A porta cabin used as a permanent or semi-permanent commercial outlet is eligible for registration under the relevant state's Shops and Establishments Act in most Indian states, provided the cabin is placed on a legally held plot — owned, leased, or licensed.

The registration application typically requires a description of the premises including dimensions, a site plan showing the structure's position on the plot, and proof of legal occupation of the land. SAMAN provides a dimensions certificate, a structural description document, and a product specification sheet with every shop cabin order — these are the documents you or your CA will submit with the Shops and Establishments application.

For food and beverage outlets operating from a porta cabin, FSSAI registration is applied for separately by the business owner. SAMAN provides the cabin's material and construction specification — confirming that the interior surfaces are suitable for food handling — as a supporting document for the FSSAI application.

Both documents are provided at handover — no separate request or additional charge.

Handover of a new SAMAN porta cabin shop — the business owner receives the dimensions certificate and structural specification document provided with every shop cabin order. These documents support Shops and Establishments Act registration and FSSAI applications. No separate request or additional charge.

For the full SAMAN porta cabin range — sizes, materials, and rental options — see our porta cabins hub page.

If your shop format requires an attached wash facility, see our porta cabin with toilet page for combined shop and washroom configurations.

Frequently Asked Questions

Can a porta cabin shop be placed on a commercial lease plot without digging a permanent foundation?

Yes. A SAMAN porta cabin shop is placed on a prepared level surface — compacted ground, a concrete plinth, or a steel grillage base — without any foundation excavation. Most commercial lease plots in India can accommodate a porta cabin without structural ground work. The cabin sits on steel skid feet and can be lifted off the surface and relocated without demolition. Confirm the ground surface type at the quote stage and we will recommend the right base arrangement.

Can a franchise company's standard brand colours and livery be exactly replicated on a porta cabin exterior?

Yes. SAMAN matches any standard RAL chart colour for the exterior cladding at the powder coat stage. For franchise brands with a specific RAL code — as most major franchises specify in their brand guidelines — we confirm the code and apply it to the order. For non-RAL brand colours, we confirm the closest RAL match and provide a colour swatch before fabrication. Large-format vinyl wrap using your franchise artwork is also available — panels are prepared for vinyl application before dispatch.

Is a porta cabin food outlet eligible for FSSAI registration in India?

Yes. A porta cabin used as a food outlet qualifies for FSSAI registration under the same categories as any small food business — a Food Business Operator licence for outlets with annual turnover below Rs 12 lakh, or a State Licence for larger operators. The material and interior surface specification of the cabin — which SAMAN provides in writing at handover — is required as part of the FSSAI application to confirm food-safe surfaces. The business operator holds the licence, not the structure.

Tell us your retail format, frontage preference, size, and branding requirements.

We will confirm the configuration, colour, lead time, and price within 24 hours.

Call or WhatsApp: +91 97089 89937
